action.hypothesis: Eight no-improve rounds indicate the current lowrank_gated_residual tuning track is exhausted, and the frontier already covers K={32,64,128}, so the next informative move must switch the single primary variable back to architecture. The cleanest fundamentally different probe is to re-benchmark `jumprelu` at K=128 under the current strongest shared recipe (`EXPANSION_FACTOR=32`, signum, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0`) to test whether a different sparse activation mechanism benefits from the stronger mainline training regime that earlier non-gated families did not fully share.
action.summary: Run a proxy comparison with `ARCHITECTURE=jumprelu` at K=128 using the current strong shared lowrank_gated_residual training recipe, changing only architecture. This satisfies the mandatory architecture pivot after repeated stagnation while avoiding new code risk and giving the only remaining incubating family a fair comparison under today's strongest stable setup.
action.self_review: This round stops the failed minor-parameter loop on `lowrank_gated_residual` and isolates a genuine architecture change with no code edits. It also uses an existing working architecture path, which is lower risk than opening another new family after recent crash and identical-to-baseline failures.
notes_to_memory: Severe stagnation forced a pivot away from further lowrank_gated_residual parameter sweeps and back to a fundamentally different architecture.
`jumprelu` is the selected comparison target because it is the only remaining incubating family with a stable existing code path and no new implementation risk this round.
This run tests whether the current strongest shared recipe changes the verdict on `jumprelu` relative to its much weaker earlier evidence.
next_hypotheses: If `jumprelu` is competitive at K=128 proxy, promote it to full before any further family-local tuning.
If `jumprelu` remains clearly behind, archive it and plan the next round around a different architecture mechanism rather than more lowrank_gated_residual micro-tuning.
If `jumprelu` shows promising quality with poor throughput, follow with a narrow performance-oriented code fix instead of discarding the architecture on quality grounds.
